posts.title: Activity Parcels
posts.content: Yesterday was a lovely day here at Woodvale Centre! We packed and delivered 80 activity parcels for our older members and members of the community 👏rnrnThese parcels included a home baked scone or cupcake (freshly baked yesterday morning), a 500 piece jigsaw puzzle, an adult colouring book with pencils, a hot chocolate sachet and the recipe for the scones that were inside some of the parcels 😀rnrnYesterday wouldn't have been possible without our wonderful staff and volunteers who baked, packed and delivered these parcels and a very special thank you goes to the FPC Foundation who gave us the funds to be able to put these parcels together! Thank you very, very much. We have had some lovely comments so far and we are very grateful for your contribution to this project  😀rnrn rnrnrnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn

posts.title: Supporting our community through COVID-19
posts.content: It has been almost 4 months since we closed our doors due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Very quickly we had to postpone all of our groups, courses and any upcoming activities planned. At the time we didn't know how long this would be for and I think we all felt quite confident that it wouldn't be long until we could welcome everyone back to the Community Centre but unfortunately that hasn't been the case.rnrnrnrnWe have been extremely busy over the past 4 months and our staff and volunteers have worked tirelessly to keep everything going to ensure we can continue to support the community. We have been very lucky to have had financial support from some wonderful funders who have helped us with some core costs and project costs too.rnrnAt the beginning of lockdown our staff and volunteers delivered flyers to lots of local houses and any businesses still open advertising that we could help people and that we would still be here doing what we could during this difficult time.rnrnVery early on we found that the main need was a shopping service and food parcels for older people and families. With the panic buying that we saw across the country local people found that they couldn't access the basic things they needed for their meals at home. We worked with Brighter Living Partnership to offer a contactless shopping service for those who who were shielding and self isolating. To date WACA have completed approximately 88 shops for local people and this is an ongoing project with many of the people we shop for continuing to shield until the end of July.rnrnThe Brunswick Youth and Community Centre generously donated a large amount of non perishable food, along with Tesco Formby (through the FareShare scheme) who we collect from weekly enabling us to provide a mixture of fresh and non perishable food parcels for local people. To date we have delivered approximately 155 parcels to local older people and families. These parcels were packed and delivered by local volunteers.rnrnrnrnAs the days and weeks passed and we continued to make weekly contact with our members and members of the community for welfare checks and to see how everyone has been coping and we discovered that a lot of people were fed up, feeling a bit low and desperate to resume their normal routines. We came up with the idea to provide these people with activity packs to keep them busy and with funding from The FPC Foundation Ainsdale and Tesco Bags of Help we were able to deliver 160 of these activity parcels to older people. They included puzzle books, jigsaws, cakes, recipes and a plant and we have been overwhelmed by the positive response we have had from these parcels. These were packed and delivered by staff and local volunteers.rnrn      rnrn       rnrnrnrnThe comments we have received from these parcels has been wonderful and we would like to share some of those with you:rnrn"How delighted I was to see you outside my door. What a nice surprise it was to see a different face, 'cos I have missed you, your team and the CBX ladies"rnrn"Many thanks once again for these gifts, and to know that at this time of uncertainty that there are people that are kind and willing to help"rnrn"Thank you so much for the parcel you have delivered, it is nice to know that we have not been forgotten"rnrn"Just a quick message to say thank you so much for my mums lovely parcel yesterday- she was really touched and it made her day. With us not living nearby It means a lot to know you are looking out for her, so thank you on behalf of my brother and I"rnrnWe have also worked closely with the Wellbeing Support Officer, Activities Co-ordinator and the National Health and Wellbeing Manager at Riverside's Retirement Living Scheme to provide activity and food parcels to the older people living in The Orchards.rnrn          rnrnWe have also had some fantastic support from local young people who have made art work, written letters and given us tokens that they have made to put in food parcels and shopping bags. This has really put a smile on everyone's face and it is wonderful to see these pieces displayed in windows and on doors as we walk and drive around the estate.rnrn    rnrnWe continue to support our community during this time of need and are here to help in any way that we can. We also work closely with many other local organisations who have fantastic services so if we are unable to support you we may know someone who can.

posts.title: Our first class back!!
posts.content: Yesterday afternoon we held our first class in 7 months, it was absolutely lovely to be back and see lots of familiar faces in the community centre!rnrnWe held a Still Life Canvas Workshop where young people were encouraged to sketch and then paint some or all of the still life objects we had set up for them.rnrnEach young person had a different interpretation and approach to this task. With some focussing on the whole image and then adding in their own creative touch to finish their painting to other focussing on one or two objects and using their colour wheels to help find contrasting colours to paint with. We were very impressed with the finished results of yesterdays class and we can't wait to see what today brings in our Macramé class!rnrn

posts.title: Macramé workshop
posts.content:
rn
Our Macramé class has just finished and we are super impressed at how quickly the young people that attended picked up the knots we taught them! Amazing work and amazing results!!
rn
rn
rn
Today we learnt a larks head knot, a left and right handed square knot and how to use these together to create a square knot pattern or individually to create a spiral, a gathering knot and how to invert our cord to create a gap/change colours and show space between patterns! An hour and a half was just enough time to make two lovely keyrings to take home
rn
rn
rn
rn
rn
rn
rn

posts.title: Bike maintenance and local bike rides
posts.content: Yesterday 8 young people learnt some amazing skills on our Bike Maintenance courses and we were super impressed with our group this morning that braved the rain for a local bike ride. We are so pleased with how successful all of the sessions were, the groups we had on each session were fantastic, we were very impressed with how much everyone learnt and how enthusiastic everyone was too. That is our half term done and we're so happy to be back up & running activities for local people!rnrn

posts.title: Macramé Rainbow Crafts
posts.content: We had so much fun making these lovely rainbow decorations! We spent some time thinking about where we would hang them up at home and what colours we could use to create that colourful rainbow.rnrn[gallery ids="2140,2137,2138,2139,2141"]rnrnHere is a time lapse video of us creating one start to finish:rnrn[video width="848" height="480" mp4="https://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2020/12/VID-20201209-WA0000.mp4"][/video]

posts.title: Thank you
posts.content:
rn
We would just like to take this opportunity to say thank you to everyone who supported us this year.
rn
rn
rn
 
rn
rn
rn
Thank you to the wonderful organisations who we work in partnership with for your help and guidance, the work that you all do is amazing!
rn
rn
rn
 
rn
rn
rn
A huge thanks goes to our incredible staff and volunteer team including our amazing Trustees who helped to shop, pack and deliver information packs, food parcels, activity parcels for older people and activity parcels for young people. The feedback from this support has been overwhelming and it has been so wonderful to hear the boost that it has given people.
rn
rn
rn
We are so grateful to be based in such a fantastic, supportive community and we look forward to working with you all again in 2021. We are closed from 23 December 2020 and will be back on 4 January 2021.
rn
rn
We hope you have a lovely break, stay safe and we wish you good health and happiness for the New Year.

posts.title: Afternoon Tea Boxes
posts.content:
We have been trying to find ways to give local people a boost and recently we delivered 40 Afternoon Tea boxes to our lunch club members. We have really missed all our groups here at the centre and wanted to do something special for everyone. We're lucky enough to say that we'll have more of these boxes coming soon too!rn
rn
These boxes were made extra special by the amazing and beautiful Spring cards from the children at Kings Meadow Primary School.
rn
rn

posts.title: The past year in art - our Young People's pandemic art project
posts.content:
Over the past couple of weeks we've been talking to young people about how they have experienced this past year. We're turning all of our ideas into a mixed media art piece that will be displayed on the youth noticeboard in the community centre. We'll update again soon when all the pieces are finished but here is a sneak peak of what has been done so far...rnrn rn
rn
rn
rn
rn
rn

posts.title: Pandemic art project
posts.content:
A sneak peek at how our pandemic art project is coming on! Our next session isn't for a couple of weeks but we are so excited to see this all finished! Our group has been working so hard on it and they are all so talented!rn
rn
rn
rn

posts.title: Zoom Decopatch Workshop
posts.content:
Today was our first Zoom Decopatch Workshop and it was amazing! So relaxing and immersive and a great opportunity to try something new and chat to one another too!rn
rn
A HUGE thank you to everyone who came this morning! We had the most wonderful time and are so grateful you were able to be there. And another HUGE thank you to Zulay for running the session, showing us this lovely craft and talking to us about the importance of self care and how relaxing crafts can be.
rn
rn

posts.title: Pandemic Art Project
posts.content: We are so impressed with the art work that our group came up with and put together for our Pandemic Art Project. This project was all about speaking to young people and hearing their experiences of this past year.rnrnWe started with a whiteboard full of notes and ideas and then grouped them together to make 6 pieces of art. The young people decided between them who would work on each piece and supported one another if they got stuck for ideas or needed help to design their piece.rnrnThis is the finished result and we are so proud of all the hard work that went in to this project and it has been really valuable to hear how young people have experienced this past year.rnrnTop left: The Start of Lockdown showing press conferences, panic buying in the supermarkets and listing more things we suddenly needed to do such as social distancing and wearing face masksrnrnBottom left: All the Apps we used on our phones during the Pandemic and the creative ways we all stayed in touch and in schoolrnrnTop middle: Showing a bubble machine releasing bubbles that show social distancing, family bubbles, candle lit service in remembrance, toilet roll and the posters we had in our windowsrnrnBottom middle: Created to look like a family noticeboard highlighting the three days we were allowed to spend together at ChristmasrnrnTop right: All the hobbies we took up during lockdown and throughout the pandemic - painting, baking, walking, music, sports etc.rnrnBottom right: All those Coronavirus emotions. From happiness to annoyance and frustration and everything in betweenrnrn

How fab does this set up look for the WACA Sports session last Friday night?!

posts.title: Thank you
Summer Sizzlers Programme 2021
posts.content: Wow what a summer!rnrnAfter missing a year due to Covid we were so excited to get back up and running our favourite activities through August. We had so much fun at Chester zoo learning about ecosystems, rock climbing, bowling, visiting Crosby lakeside for water-sports and more.rnrnAs you can see from our pictures we had such a fun time. Bring on next summer!rnrn[gallery ids="2627,2628,2629,2630,2632,2633,2634"]

pages.title: Home
pages.content:
Woodvale and Ainsdale Community Association (WACA) is a registered charity in Woodvale, Southport. We provide daytime, evening and school holiday activities for people in the local community.

rn

We have two centres within the area for the community to use, subject to availability. Our Community Centre is located on Meadow Lane and our Recreation Centre is on Orchard Lane backing on to the local recreation fields.

rn

We pride ourselves on being able to provide quality services for all members of the community. If you find anything that is not up to standard, or you would like to leave us positive feedback, don't hesitate to get in touch!

rnrn

To help support our Charity please see our Wishlist link below:

rn

WACA's Amazon Wishlist

rn[video poster="https://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2019/01/20180626_124811.jpg" width="848" height="480" mp4="https://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2021/04/com-centre-vid.mp4" autoplay="true"][/video]rn

pages.title: Short Courses
pages.content: We are always running short courses funded by different companies. Select a course from our drop down menu and if you would like to book onto anything please get in touch with us.

pages.content:
We're here to answer any questions or queries you may have and look forward to hearing from you. If you call us and we're not in, just leave us a message. We'll get back to you as soon as we can.rn

pages.title: Our Board of Trustees
pages.content:
rnWe are currently advertising for more people to become Trustees at Woodvale and Ainsdale Community Association. If you are interested please contact the Community Centre for more information.rnrnThe directors of the company are also charity trustees for the purposes of charity law and under the company’s articles are known as members of the Management Committee. All member of the Management Committee give their time voluntarily and received no benefits from the charity.rnrnWoodvale & Ainsdale Community Association is managed by the elected trustees, the Chair, Vice Chair, Treasurer, and other trustees, together with the Community Centre Manager, and Administrator who meet on a monthly basis.rn

The last Annual General Meeting was held in 2024. At the meeting Trustees retire by rotation, starting with those who have been longest in office since their last appointment, in accordance with the Articles of the Association.

rn

The current board is shown below:

rnrn
    rn
  1. Chair: Alison Marshall
    2. rn
  2. Vice Chair: Amy Suthren
    3. rn
  3. Treasurer: Tom Boynton
    4. rn
  4. Trustee: Anne Burman
    5. rn
  5. Trustee: Lynne Thompson
    6. rn
  6. Trustee: Lindsay Pendleton
    7. rn
  7. Trustee: Arianne Clarke
    8. rn
rn

The day to day management of the Charity is delegated to the Community Centre Manager, who in turn, leads a staff team with specific areas of responsibility.

rnPlease visit our Meet the Team page for more information.

pages.title: Background
pages.content:
In 1992, local Woodvale residents identified a lack of both a community focal point and co-ordinated activities. Initially the group met in a disused school, but in 1999 (having secured charitable status) residents convinced the borough council to provide a purpose built community centre on the original site of the school.

rn

Woodvale & Ainsdale Community Association (WACA) established itself as a charitable organisation based in the Woodvale area of Ainsdale, Southport. The new centre was as an integral part of a scheme to build a new housing estate and primary school for the area.

rnThis area is covered by the Middle Super output area Sefton 011. It is an isolated and deprived estate which is surrounded by far more prosperous areas.rn
    rn
  • Sefton 011B is within Ainsdale ward and one of 189 LSOAs in Sefton local authority district.
    • rn
  • According to the 2019 Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD), this particular area – Sefton 011B LSOA E01006924 ranks 3,523 out of 32,844 LSOAs (where a rank of 1 is the most deprived LSOA).
    • rn
  • In 2019, this LSOA ranked within the 10% most deprived neighbourhoods in the country on the income domain, the employment domain and the health deprivation and disability domain
    • rn
  • In 2015 this LSOA was ranked within the 20% most deprived neighbourhoods in the country for Income Deprivation Affecting Children and by 2019 the deprivation has increased showing the area ranking within the 10% most deprived
    • rn
  • The Income Deprivation Affecting Older People is mostly unchanged and Sefton 011B is ranked within the 10% most deprived neighbourhoods in the country for this.
    • rn
rnSource:  (http://dclgapps.communities.gov.uk/imd/iod_index.html#)rn

According to the Sefton Health Profile (2013), deprivation in the area is higher than average and 9800 children in Sefton live in poverty. There are different deprivation levels throughout Sefton, and Woodvale suffers with much higher levels of deprivation than the surrounding areas, with 1735 households suffering deprivation. Long term unemployment is much worse in Sefton (14.4%) than the average for England (9.5%)

rn

WACA “area of benefit” has since increased to the boundaries of Ainsdale, with priority given to the deprived communities of Woodvale.

pages.title: Forms
pages.content: If you would like to attend activities at our centre please complete the relevant form and bring it with you to the class. If you do not have a printer or you would like help to complete the form, don't worry! Pop in to the centre and we're more than happy to help you before your class starts.rnrnFor Summer activities please ensure that young people have a consent form completed in full by a parent or guardian. This form must be returned to the community centre before the activity day. If we're not in, just pop it in an envelope in our letterbox next to the front door and we'll pick it up on the next working day.rnrnTo download, please click on the form. Alternatively, come to our community centre to pick up a copy of the form you need.rnrnAdult Consent and Medical Form 2026rnrnUnder 18 Consent and Medical Form 2026rnrn

Our staff and volunteers are managed and supervised by the Centre Manager & Deputy Manager, who, in turn is managed and supervised by the trustees. All necessary staff and volunteers have enhanced DBS disclosures in place.

pages.title: Chair Based Exercise
pages.content: rnrnThe Chair Based Exercise class is one of our most popular classes here at the centre, and no wonder! It's a very bubbly, fun class with the opportunity to catch up over a cuppa after the class.rnrnThis is a great class for anyone who would like to improve their mobility, gain confidence and improve their balance.rnrnBooking is essential for this class and bookings will be taken one week in advance of the class. Please ring 01704 573084 to book your place.rnrn[video width="640" height="352" mp4="http://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2018/07/VID-20180710-WA0001Trim.mp4"][/video]rnrn

pages.title: Craft Group
pages.content: Our Craft Group is the perfect opportunity to meet new friends whilst having a cuppa and getting crafty.rnrnPlease note this is a self led group, so no materials or instructors included. rnrnBooking is essential and you must book to avoid disappointment. You will be able to book one week in advance for each session.rnrn

pages.title: Volunteer
pages.content:
Volunteers

rnWe are always looking for more volunteers to get involved in the different projects we run each week. From helping in the Woodlands and Community garden, preparing teas and coffees for classes, driving our minibus or cooking at our afternoon lunches there's always plenty to get involved in. You may have other skills that could be utilised by the Charity so please get in touch if you would like to Volunteer.  Volunteer Application Formrn

Board of Trustees

rnWe are looking for people aged 18+ who care about the local community and would like to make a difference. The Trustees meet once a month and contribute to the planning and development of the Charity. We are looking for people to compliment their skills. If you have a few hours to spare each month in the evening and feel that you would like to get involved please contact us for an application form and more details.rnrn[gallery size="medium" ids="2296,2297"]

pages.title: Our Covid-19 Safety Measures
pages.content:
Things may be a little different for a while, so please bear with us. To help create a safe environment for everyone, we’re enhancing our high standards of cleaning even further. There will also be some changes to our normal style of service to ensure everyone is safe but we can guarantee that you will still get a warm welcome from the team here at Woodvale Centre.rnrnHere is what we are doing in our Centres:rn
    rn
  • Hand sanitiser is available in all spaces throughout the building including at the entrance
    • rn
  • We are regularly deep cleaning both centres
    • rn
  • Hygiene stations are placed in all bathrooms to allow members to clean areas they use before and after if they wish and antibacterial soap is available for hand washing in each bathroom with disposable paper towels
    • rn
  • All refreshments will be made and served by a member of staff or a trained volunteer to limit access to the kitchen
    • rn
rnWhat you can do when you visit our centres:rnrnIf you are feeling unwell or have developed symptoms of Covid-19 please stay at home, we will still be here when you are well enough to attend again.rn
    rn
  • If you would like to attend an activity or visit the centre please book in advance or call us to make arrangements – this is very important to ensure we do not exceed group maximums, we don’t like to turn people away so please call us first
    • rn
  • Wash your hands or use hand sanitiser as you enter the building and before you leave
    • rn
  • Catch it and bin it with all your coughs and sneezes and wash your hands afterwards
    • rn
  • Follow the guidance that staff offer at the start of each group, we are doing this to help keep everyone safe
    • rn
rnIn addition:rn
    rn
  • We cannot host members between sessions, we need to leave enough time between groups to clean and disinfect high traffic areas and we cannot allow members to stay on site whilst they wait for another group to start. 
    • rn

pages.title: Get Involved
pages.content: Here at Woodvale Community Centre we love hearing from local residents that they would like to help out! rnrnWhether you would like to donate, volunteer or fund-raise please get in touch with us here

pages.title: Alison and Elle's Skydive
pages.content: Alison and Elle took the plunge on the 19th of October and jumped out of a plane from 14,000ft up! They have tirelessly fundraised for our Charity and we are so grateful that they felt able to do this Sky Dive for us.rnrnA huge thank you to both of them, you are incredible! We are still totalling up how much was raised but we will update you as soon as it’s all in and counted but we know at this moment that Alison and Elle have raised over £1,000 for Woodvale Centre!rnrn[video poster="http://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2019/10/thumbnail_Alison-097.jpg" width="1280" height="720" mp4="http://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2019/10/TandemSkydive.mp4"][/video]rnrn[video poster="http://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2019/10/thumbnail_Nicky-Eleanor-069.jpg" width="1920" height="1080" mp4="http://www.woodvalecommunitycentre.org/app/uploads/2019/10/NickyEleanorvideo.mp4"][/video]
